judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent and correct on all four expectations, with nearly identical command choices and reasoning; Report 2 is marginally more thorough (explicit blob-hash checks, test suite reference) but this doesn't change expectation coverage.

report_1.evidence: Base branch: `main`. ... Targeting `feat/weighted-scorer` would base the PR on a dead branch; `main` is the only live integration target.

report_2.evidence: The PR should target **`main`**, not `feat/weighted-scorer`, even though the branch was physically built on top of `feat/weighted-scorer`.

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was created from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (`ac9d505`, "W3: token-overlap helper in metrics")... `feat/weighted-scorer` was **squash-merged** into `main` as PR #42 (`1971f08`).

report_2.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` (`1bfecb6` "X2", `7b2e100` "X1") was created from the tip of **`feat/weighted-scorer`** (`ac9d505` "W3")... `feat/weighted-scorer` was **squash-merged into main** as `1971f08` (#42).

report_1.evidence: `scoring/scorer.py`: the full refactor from flat to weighted metrics — new `WEIGHTS` dict, weighted `total` (+13/−3) ... The `scorer.py` refactor and the `overlap()` function belong to the weighted-scorer work (W1–W3). They appear in the PR diff purely because the merge base (`65204ad`) predates them and the squash-merge (`1971f08`) gave that content new SHAs on `main`

report_2.evidence: it is **not** all pairwise work: all of `scoring/scorer.py` and 6 of the 10 lines in `scoring/metrics.py` (the `overlap` helper and weighted-aggregation changes) are the W1–W3 weighted-scorer changes, present only because the branch forked from `65204ad`, before the squash landed.

report_1.evidence: Rebase only the pairwise commits onto main**, explicitly skipping the W commits: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... **Verify the result**: `git log --oneline main..feat/pairwise-metric` should show exactly two commits (X1, X2), and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` should now equal the 5-line pairwise-only diff.

report_2.evidence: **Transplant only the pairwise commits onto current main**: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... **Verify the rebased branch**: `git log main..feat/pairwise-metric --oneline` should list exactly two commits, and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` should now equal the 5-line content diff (2 files, 5 insertions) with `scoring/scorer.py` absent
